prompt: /prose:compress Use at most 24 words and one section.
input:
## Migration safety

The migration is safe because the old column remains during rollout.

## Rollout

Keeping the old column until every instance runs the new code makes the migration safe.

We propose serializing token refresh. Two requests can currently refresh at the same time. The second can overwrite the first token and log the user out. We will place refresh behind the existing single-flight lock. The main rollout risk is increased wait time during an identity-provider slowdown.

manual_checks: the concurrency cause and rollout risk remain accurate

prompt: Replace vague evaluation with the observable effect.
input: The retry change is a powerful reliability improvement: transient writes now retry instead of dropping.

## Retry policy

The worker retries each failed write three times.

## Failure handling

After three failed retries, the worker sends the write to the dead-letter queue.
Call AuthClient.refreshToken() once under mutex refresh_mu. See https://example.test/auth. If it returns E_REVOKED, stop and preserve the existing session.
